title: Emergent temporal abstractions in autoregressive models enable hierarchical reinforcement learning
abstract: Large-scale autoregressive models pretrained on next-token prediction and finetuned with reinforcement learning (RL) have achieved unprecedented success on many problem domains. During RL, these models explore by generating new outputs, one token at a time. However, sampling actions token-by-token can result in highly inefficient learning, particularly when rewards are sparse. Here, we show that it is possible to overcome this problem by acting and exploring within the internal representations of an autoregressive model. Specifically, to discover temporally-abstract actions, we introduce a higher-order, non-causal sequence model whose outputs control the residual stream activations of a base autoregressive model. On grid world and MuJoCo-based tasks with hierarchical structure, we find that the higher-order model learns to compress long activation sequence chunks onto internal controllers. Critically, each controller executes a sequence of behaviorally meaningful actions that unfold over long timescales and are accompanied with a learned termination condition, such that composing multiple controllers over time leads to efficient exploration on novel tasks. We show that direct internal controller reinforcement, a process we term "internal RL", enables learning from sparse rewards in cases where standard RL finetuning fails. Our results demonstrate the benefits of latent action generation and reinforcement in autoregressive models, suggesting internal RL as a promising avenue for realizing hierarchical RL within foundation models.
summary:
# 论文总结：Emergent temporal abstractions in autoregressive models enable hierarchical reinforcement learning

## 1. 核心创新点

本文最关键的创新，是把预训练 autoregressive 模型的 residual stream 直接当成可强化学习的 latent action space：先用一个 future-conditioned metacontroller 在内部发现带终止条件的 temporally-abstract actions，再在这些内部控制器上做 RL。它解决的是 sparse-reward、长时程、层次化任务里 token-by-token 探索几乎失效、credit assignment 极差的问题。

## 2. 技术创新拆解

- **残差流可控化**：在 frozen backbone 的中层插入低秩线性 controller，只改 residual stream，不改输出层；实验表明抽象动作可以被线性读出，也能被线性写回，而且中层最容易形成有效控制。
- **无监督时序分段**：提出无监督变分 metacontroller，结合 acausal sequence embedding、GRU 历史状态和 sparse switching gate β_t，无需逐步 subgoal 标签就能学到何时切换控制器。
- **可组合抽象动作**：同一个 latent controller code 可以跨场景、跨时间点重复使用，并按顺序组合成更长的 subgoal chain，直接带来 length generalization 和 compositional generalization。
- **内部 RL**：把 RL 从 raw action space 改到 controller-code space z，并把 base autoregressive model 视作环境的一部分；这样同时缩小动作维度和时间跨度，使 sparse-reward 任务可优化。

## 3. 方法论突破

- **新颖性**：与标准 RL finetuning 或 CompILE 这类方法的本质区别在于，它不是在输出动作层面直接学 policy，也不是从原始轨迹上硬做 option discovery，而是先依托预训练 backbone 的内部表示学习可复用的 latent options，再在这个抽象层面上进行强化学习。换句话说，它把 hierarchical RL 的搜索空间从原始动作转成了内部控制器空间。
- **关键技术**：核心机制是 residual-stream intervention U_t、future-conditioned variational encoder、hypernetwork decoder，以及控制时间切换的 β_t；其中 frozen backbone 保持了预训练得到的抽象结构，β_t 负责把连续 proposal 压成稀疏切换的时间分段。
- **理论支撑**：有较完整的 ELBO/rate-distortion 推导，以及 internal RL 相对 raw-action RL 的 policy gradient variance 分析，用来解释为什么冻结 backbone、降低时间粒度会提升可学性；但文中没有给出严格的收敛性或全局最优证明。

## 4. 实验验证

- **主要基准/场景**：主要在 gridworld-pinpad 和 ant-pinpad 两个层次化任务上验证，分别对应离散网格导航和 MuJoCo ant 连续控制；训练用较短的 pretraining subgoal 序列，测试用更长、重组后的 OOD 子目标序列。
- **性能提升**：线性 probe 对 latent subgoal 的解码准确率在中层附近大约从 30% 升到 50%；手工注入 go to blue latent code 后，成功率从 23% 提升到 36%；OOD transition 从 10% 提升到 24%；标准 raw-action RL、CompILE、co-training baseline 和 forced-reset baseline 在 100 万个 episode 内基本都失败，而 internal RL 在两种环境上都能学成。文中没有给出一个统一的绝对平均提升数值，但明确把该方法描述为 many orders of magnitude faster。元控制器的 β_t 还基本恢复了 ground-truth subgoal 切换时刻，并呈准二值切换。
- **消融实验**：最关键的组件是 temporal integration gate β_t 和 frozen pretrained backbone 的组合。强制 β_t=1 会破坏时间抽象，模型虽然一开始更容易拿到成功轨迹，但无法继续优化；而把 base model 和 metacontroller 共同训练会把结构塌缩成序列开头的单次切换，说明真正起作用的是稀疏切换的层次结构，而不是单纯增加控制器容量。中层插入 controller 也明显优于过浅或过深的位置。

## 5. 局限与启发

- **当前局限**：实验只在结构化、可分解的网格和 MuJoCo 任务上完成，尚未证明能直接迁移到开放式语言、真实机器人或更杂乱的 sparse-reward 场景；metacontroller 对更干净的 demonstrations 更敏感，噪声变大时抽象压缩会变差；方法还依赖可以访问并干预 residual stream 的预训练 backbone。
- **未来方向**：下一步最自然的是把 internal RL 扩展到更大规模 foundation models、LLM reasoning 和真实控制系统，并研究如何把学到的 switch 和 controller codes 蒸馏成更轻量的 causal policy；更深层的多级 options、更多 recurrence，以及与大型模型的对接，都是直接延伸方向。
- **可迁移性**：原则上可迁移到任何具有类似 residual 表示的 autoregressive 或 sequence model，只要任务本身存在可复用的层次结构，并且 backbone 已经学到可线性读出的 temporal abstractions；如果任务没有明显子目标分解，或者预训练表示不稳定，这种收益就不保证成立。

## 6. 一句话总结

它证明了 autoregressive 模型的内部残差流可以被组织成可复用、可切换的抽象动作，并且把 RL 提升到这个 latent option 层后，原本几乎不可探索的 sparse-reward 层次任务就变成了可优化问题。

recognized_fields.title: Emergent temporal abstractions in autoregressive models enable hierarchical reinforcement learning
recognized_fields.abstract: Large-scale autoregressive models pretrained on next-token prediction and finetuned with reinforcement learning (RL) have achieved unprecedented success on many problem domains. During RL, these models explore by generating new outputs, one token at a time. However, sampling actions token-by-token can result in highly inefficient learning, particularly when rewards are sparse. Here, we show that it is possible to overcome this problem by acting and exploring within the internal representations of an autoregressive model. Specifically, to discover temporally-abstract actions, we introduce a higher-order, non-causal sequence model whose outputs control the residual stream activations of a base autoregressive model. On grid world and MuJoCo-based tasks with hierarchical structure, we find that the higher-order model learns to compress long activation sequence chunks onto internal controllers. Critically, each controller executes a sequence of behaviorally meaningful actions that unfold over long timescales and are accompanied with a learned termination condition, such that composing multiple controllers over time leads to efficient exploration on novel tasks. We show that direct internal controller reinforcement, a process we term "internal RL", enables learning from sparse rewards in cases where standard RL finetuning fails. Our results demonstrate the benefits of latent action generation and reinforcement in autoregressive models, suggesting internal RL as a promising avenue for realizing hierarchical RL within foundation models.
